name: Georgie Auld
abstract: Georgie Auld (May 19, 1919 – January 8, 1990) was a Canadian jazz tenor saxophonist, clarinetist, and bandleader.

concerts.label: Carnegie Pop Concert: Jazz at the Philharmonic
Jazz Concert by Benny Goodman and Louis Armstrong
Midnight Performance: Jazz Concert by Benny Goodman and Louis Armstrong

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: I went with Georgie Auld. I played with Georgie Auld in a number of bands in the spring of ‘46, summer of ‘46
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Monk Montgomery
about_quotes.evidence: I stayed with Georgie Auld about three months... the job with Georgie Auld swung every night.

about_quotes.speaker: Pete Jolly
about_quotes.evidence: So I got to work with Jack Teagarden, Georgie Auld... So Georgie Auld and Barney Kessel called me and said, 'We have a gig out here in the valley in Studio City, do you want to come over on the fourth of July and work?'
about_quotes.doc_title: Pete Jolly interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Buddy Tate
about_quotes.evidence: George and I was always tight, you know? Georgie always -- I'll show you, on the other hand -- he calls me Miss Tate and I call him Mrs. Auld.

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: Then I had a band there for about six months with Georgie Auld. We had a band.

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Shew
about_quotes.evidence: the band was like it was an all-star band, Georgie Auld was on the band
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Shew interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 1, 2020
